steps.title: Identify the support target
steps.content: The underlined claim is not just that Baltasar and Blimunda has folklore or that Saramago was influenced by magical realism. It says the novel resembles classic magical realist novels by juxtaposing literary realism with Portuguese folklore. The best quotation must therefore show realistic representation and folkloric or antirealistic elements interacting inside the novel itself.

steps.title: Compare the quotations
steps.content: A says realistic and antirealistic modes alternate and that folklore appears throughout, but it does not clearly explain how the two modes work against each other. B mainly names Portuguese folklore and Latin American magical realism as influences. D also describes influence, saying the novel is indebted to Portuguese folklore. C is stronger because it says the logic of the realistic plot is repeatedly disrupted by imagery and situations from Portuguese folklore, directly showing the claimed juxtaposition. The answer is C.
